# GS01 — Hermes routing of verify findings

Archetype `gallery_shooter` (Space-Invaders / fixed-axis), companion-mode run at `out/gs01/`.
Harness verdicts: **M1 VALIDATION_PASSED (9/9)** · **M2 VALIDATION_FAILED** (fidelity 3/4 — M2-A3 bounded-score) · **M3 throws uncaught, mislabeled "boot failed"** (no `report.M3.json` was written; the `M3-A{1..4}-fail.png` exist from the original run). Both failures reproduced read-only against `out/gs01/dist`.

## Routing summary

| # | Finding | Root (one phrase) | Source owner (file/skill/node) | Chain-or-node | Local vs promote | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | M2-A3 `score <= maxScore` fails with `maxScore===0` | gallery_shooter module ships **no scorable-placing system** that calls `registerScorable`, so the engine-accumulated `maxScore` registry key is never written (stays 0) | `templates/modules/gallery_shooter/src/**` (per-archetype registry/module code — the `archetype-registry` system) | node (per-archetype engine) — wire the existing `registerScorable` seam at member placement | **LOCAL** to the gallery_shooter module | High (reproduced; W4 self-note + code agree) |
| 2 | M3 boot crash `TypeError: ...reading 'entries'` | hook `collectEntities` calls `group.getChildren()` on a Phaser Group **destroyed by `scene.restart()`**; the `typeof getChildren !== 'function'` guard does not detect a destroyed group (`this.children` is null) | `templates/core/src/hook.ts:138-139` (shared 2D engine hook — the `game-omni` core) | node (shared engine seam) — the destroyed-group window is real; M3-A2's `event` verb routes the harness through `commands.reset()` | **LOCAL** to `templates/core` (the shared 2D hook) | High (reproduced 6/6 in the reset window) |

> Anti-reward-hack note for BOTH: the verify harness, the `__GAME__` hook's PUBLIC SHAPE, the `gdd`/blueprint assertions, and the `score.ts` contract are the IMMUTABLE oracle. Neither route touches them — both fix real `src/**` engine behavior. (Two harness robustness observations are recorded as a separate cross-cutting note; they are NOT the route for these findings.)

---

## Finding 1 — M2-A3 bounded score fails because `maxScore` is never accumulated

- **Symptom** — `out/gs01/verify/report.M2.json` → `VALIDATION_FAILED`, summary "score never exceeds the bounded max (expected score <= 0; got 1)". Assertion **M2-A3** (`observe:"score"`, `comparator:"atMost"`, `expected:0`, `observed:1`, `status:"fail"`). The other three M2 fidelity checks pass (A1 score increases 0→60, A2 idempotent, A4 formation=53). The W4-M2 node self-documented the cause in `out/gs01/MEMORY.w4-M2.md` ("Critical discovery": gallery_shooter `spawnMember` does not call `utils.registerScorable`).

- **Trace** (backward from the failing comparator):
  1. The comparator reads `__GAME__.maxScore` → `out/gs01/src/hook.ts:263-268` `get maxScore() { return num(game.registry.get('maxScore'), 0); }` — reads the `'maxScore'` registry key, defaulting to **0** when absent.
  2. The ONLY writer of `'maxScore'` is `registerScorable` → `templates/core-contract/src/score.ts:75-80` (`host.registry.set('maxScore', next)`). Its own doc (score.ts:16-23, 64-74): "`maxScore` is ENGINE-ACCUMULATED … the system that PLACES rewards calls this once per reward it places."
  3. Score CREDIT works (M2-A1 passes): `BaseGameScene.onEnemyKilled` (`out/gs01/src/scenes/BaseGameScene.ts:275-285`) reads `enemy.__points` and calls `utils.addScore`. But CREDIT (`addScore`) ≠ CEILING (`registerScorable`) — two different registry keys.
  4. Members are placed in `DataShooterScene.spawnMember`; the template seam `templates/modules/gallery_shooter/src/scenes/DataShooterScene.ts:260` does `sprite.__points = tmpl.points;` and **never** calls `registerScorable(this, sprite.__points)`.
  5. Confirmed module-wide: `grep registerScorable|maxScore|resetMaxScore templates/modules/gallery_shooter/src/` → **zero hits**. The gallery_shooter module has NO scorable-placing system (no analogue of platformer's `CollectScore`). Contrast the platformer, which DOES wire it: `templates/modules/platformer/src/systems/CollectScore.ts:88` (`utils.resetMaxScore` in `reset()`) + `:114` (`utils.registerScorable(scene, this.valuePerReward)` per placed reward).
  - Net: any kill makes `score` (credited) `> maxScore` (stuck at 0), so `score atMost maxScore` fails the instant score>0.

- **Root cause** — M2-A3 fails because the gallery_shooter archetype module never registers its formation members' point values into the `maxScore` ceiling — it ships the score-CREDIT path but omits the score-CEILING accumulation that the shared `score.ts` contract requires a placing system to call.

- **Owner / route (Hermes law 4)** — **`templates/modules/gallery_shooter/src/**`** (the `archetype-registry` tracked system — per-archetype engine/module code; map `.agents/archetype-registry-map.md`). The fix wires the EXISTING `registerScorable`/`resetMaxScore` seam from the shared `core-contract/score.ts` into the gallery_shooter member-placement path — the same wiring platformer's `CollectScore` already has. This is the SOURCE owner, not a downstream guard: VERIFY-2's bounded self-fix (the W4 note's "patch in VERIFY-2") would be a per-run patch of generated `out/<id>/src`, which (a) does not generalize to future gallery_shooter runs and (b) in companion mode is skipped entirely. The durable home is the template module every future run scaffolds from. It is NOT a W1/HARDEN authoring gap: the design DELIBERATELY makes `maxScore` an engine-DERIVED runtime symbol (score.ts:16-23) rather than an authored integer, so HARDEN authoring nothing here is CORRECT — the gap is purely the engine wiring.

- **Local-first vs promote** — **LOCAL.** The generality test (playbooks §"generality test"): the rule names this repo's archetype (`gallery_shooter`) and its placement seam — it is a BINDING, stays in the module. The shared METHOD (the `registerScorable`/`resetMaxScore` contract) already exists upstream in `templates/core-contract/score.ts` and needs no change; only the gallery_shooter module must CALL it. No upstream skill owns "wire score into a new archetype," so nothing promotes.

- **Generalization + anti-reward-hack** — Holds for ALL future gallery_shooter runs: the formation/wave score ceiling becomes the real Σ of placed member point values for every game of this archetype. It changes real engine behavior (writes the `maxScore` registry key at placement); it does NOT touch the assertion, the `gdd` milestone tail, the `__GAME__` hook shape, or `score.ts` (the immutable oracle). The HUD format `x/max` (declared in `spec/shell.fragment.json`) also starts resolving correctly as a side benefit.

- **Suggested fix direction** (NOT applied; smallest durable edit) — Add a scorable-placing system to the gallery_shooter module (the `CollectScore` analogue), OR, more minimally, have the formation builder register the ceiling once per placement: call `utils.registerScorable(this, sprite.__points)` in `DataShooterScene.spawnMember` after line 260, and `utils.resetMaxScore(this)` once before the formation is (re)built / on the WaveLoop re-spawn seam (so a re-attached/replayed wave does not double-count, exactly as `CollectScore.reset()` does at platformer's restart). Verify against the contract's idempotency note (score.ts:71-74).

---

## Finding 2 — M3 crash: `getChildren()` on a group destroyed by `scene.restart()`

- **Symptom** — `out/gs01/verify/` has NO `report.M3.json` (only `M3-A{1..4}-fail.png`). Re-running `npx tsx bin/verify-milestone.ts out/gs01 M3` prints `VALIDATION_FAILED: game did not become ready (boot failed): verify runner error: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'entries')` with the minified stack `initialize.getChildren → Ie → get entities`. M1 boots+passes, M2 boots+reports (with the SAME error noted as a *non-blocking* perturbation error in `report.M2.json` `consoleErrors`) — only M3's run throws uncaught.

- **Trace** (backward from the crash):
  1. Minified `getChildren` decoded from `out/gs01/dist/assets/index-FEjg0VLB.js:1527` = **Phaser** `Group.getChildren: function(){ return this.children.entries }`. So `this.children` is **undefined/null** on the group → reading `.entries` throws. (Not the app's `BaseGameScene.ts:230` `.children.entries`; the stack's `get entities` proves it is the HOOK getter path.)
  2. `Ie` = the hook's `collectEntities`, reached from `get entities()` → `out/gs01/src/hook.ts:274-276` (`collectEntities(s, s.player)`), which at `hook.ts:136-139` iterates known group names and calls `group.getChildren()`. The guard at **`hook.ts:138`** is `if (!group || typeof group.getChildren !== 'function') continue;` — a **destroyed** Phaser Group still HAS `getChildren` on its prototype, so the guard passes and `getChildren()` (hook.ts:139) throws when `this.children` is null. (gs01's `src/hook.ts` collectEntities block is byte-identical to the shared `templates/core/src/hook.ts:120-156`.)
  3. WHO destroys the group: M3-A2's input is `event: overlap:hazard,player`. The harness verb-driver `templates`-side equivalent `packages/verify/src/drivers-2d.ts` routes this to `driveHazard` (drivers-2d.ts:197), whose FIRST action is `resetLevel(page)` (drivers-2d.ts:198) → `commands.reset()` (drivers-2d.ts:91) → the hook's `commands.reset` (`scene.restart()`). `driveHazard` then reads `__GAME__.entities` (drivers-2d.ts:202) to resolve obstacle ids — i.e. it reads entities DURING the restart window.
  4. Phaser `scene.restart()` shuts the scene down (`Group.destroy()` nulls `this.children`) before `create()` rebuilds the groups. **Reproduced read-only** (boot `out/gs01/dist`, fire `commands.reset()`, then read `__GAME__.entities`): the read throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'entries')` at +0/+10/+30/+60/+120/+250 ms (6/6) — the destroyed-group window persists across the whole shutdown→recreate.
  5. WHY uncaught (→ mislabeled "boot failed"): the assertion read path `readObserve`/`driveEvent` (`packages/verify/src/compile.ts:95-100`) is NOT `.catch`-guarded; the throw propagates out of `runMilestoneVerify2` to the CLI catch-all `bin/verify-milestone.ts:114-115` which formats ANY thrown error as `formatBootFailed("verify runner error: …")` and writes NO report. M2 hit the identical crash only inside the perturbation gate, which IS wrapped (`harness.ts:713-719`) → recorded non-blocking → report still written. **M3-A2 is the only assertion across M1/M2/M3 whose input routes through `resetLevel`** (verified: M1 = none/keyHold; M2 = `overlap:projectile,enemy` → the live 2D-overlap-proxy in `driveEvent`, no reset; M3-A2 = `overlap:hazard,player` → `driveHazard` → reset), which is why only M3 surfaces it uncaught.

- **Root cause** — The M3 boot crash is `collectEntities` reading `__GAME__.entities` during a `scene.restart()` window, because the hook's group guard (`typeof getChildren !== 'function'`) does not detect a Phaser Group whose internal `children` Set was nulled by `Group.destroy()` — so `getChildren()` throws on `this.children.entries`.

- **Owner / route (Hermes law 4)** — **`templates/core/src/hook.ts:136-153`** (the shared 2D engine hook — the `game-omni` core, not a per-archetype module; `collectEntities` and the `groupNames` loop are generic across all five 2D archetypes). The route hardens the entity-collection read so reading `__GAME__.entities` is SAFE for ANY caller at ANY scene-lifecycle moment (a read-only observer must never throw mid-restart). This is the chain/contract owner, NOT a downstream guard: the alternative reactive guards — wrapping `readObserve` in `.catch` (compile.ts), adding a settle to `resetLevel`/`driveHazard` (drivers-2d.ts), or broadening the CLI catch-all — all live in the IMMUTABLE harness and would only paper over a hook that throws on a legitimate read. The contract is "`__GAME__` is a thin, READ-ONLY, never-throws adapter" (hook.ts:29-48: "a field that doesn't apply is `undefined`, never throws"); the violation is in the hook, so the hook is the owner. Because gs01's hook is byte-identical to `templates/core/src/hook.ts`, fixing the template fixes every future 2D run.

- **Local-first vs promote** — **LOCAL to `templates/core`.** Generality test: the rule is about the shared 2D hook's group-read safety — it does NOT name an archetype, so it is genre-AGNOSTIC and belongs in the one shared 2D hook (where it benefits platformer/top_down/paddle_ball/endless_runner/gallery_shooter alike). It is still product-local (`game-omni`'s `templates/core`), not an upstream global skill — no upstream skill owns "engine hook entity-read." So it stays in `templates/core/src/hook.ts`; it does not promote.

- **Generalization + anti-reward-hack** — A robustness fix in the shared hook makes `__GAME__.entities` safe for every archetype and every harness gate (assertion read, invariant sampler, perturbation, completability) under any scene-lifecycle state — it restores the documented never-throws contract. It changes real engine-seam behavior (the hook read), touching neither the assertion/oracle, the harness gates, nor the `__GAME__` public shape. It does not mask a build defect: a destroyed-group read returning `[]`/skipping that group is the CORRECT observation of a scene mid-restart (no live entities to report).

- **Suggested fix direction** (NOT applied; smallest durable edit) — In `templates/core/src/hook.ts` `collectEntities` (and the sibling `applyForce` at hook.ts:189), make the guard lifecycle-safe: skip a group whose backing children Set is gone (e.g. guard `group.children` / its `entries` before iterating) OR read children defensively so a destroyed/restarting group contributes nothing instead of throwing. The minimal, generalizing form is a single safe accessor used by both call sites (hook.ts:138-139 and 189-190); re-mirror the byte-identical block into generated `src/hook.ts` on the next scaffold. (Optionally NOTE — not the route — `resetLevel` (drivers-2d.ts:90-96) could also settle past the restart window, but that is the immutable harness; the durable fix is the hook.)

## Cross-cutting note (observations, NOT the route for either finding above)

Two harness-side weaknesses surfaced while tracing Finding 2; they MASK/MISLABEL, they are not the root, and per law 4 the routes above are the source fixes — recorded here only so a future pass can decide whether to harden the harness's diagnosis quality:
1. **Mislabeling** — the CLI catch-all `bin/verify-milestone.ts:114-115` reports ANY uncaught runner exception as "game did not become ready (boot failed)", which is misleading (the M3 game boots fine; it crashes at assertion time) and writes no report, losing per-assertion evidence. A more honest marker (e.g. `runner-error`/`harness-error` distinct from `boot-failed`) would have pointed straight at the real crash.
2. **Silent same-bug divergence** — the identical `.entries` crash was a hard FAIL in M3 (uncaught assertion read) but a "non-blocking" note in M2 (caught perturbation, `harness.ts:713-719`); the inconsistency hid that M2 and M3 share one root.
These are JUDGING-surface/diagnosis-quality issues in the immutable harness, kept OUT of the two routes (which fix product `src/**`). File against the harness only if diagnosis-quality is itself prioritized.


---

## Update — fixes applied (2026-06-25, branch fix/companion-run-findings)

- **Finding 1 (M2 maxScore=0) — FIXED** by `de7dc7b` fix(gallery_shooter): `registerScorable` per placed member + `resetMaxScore` on level-rebuild (utils + DataShooterScene). Proven: maxScore 0→1650, M2-A3 passes.
- **Finding 2 (M3 boot crash) — FIXED** by `9e5ede0` fix(core): `safeGetChildren` guard in `templates/core/src/hook.ts` (never-throws read). Proven via A/B probe (baseline throws, fixed entities=84); M3 boots + writes report.M3.json.
- **Residual same-class finding (out of original route) — FIXED** by `05c517a`: `BaseGameScene.ts:230 checkWinCondition` read `this.enemies.children.entries` directly (throws mid `scene.restart()`); now no-ops the win check while the group is gone (NOT treat-as-empty, which would false-win). Build green.
- **Harness observation (env, NOT a code/route target):** the isomorphic-perturbation gate is LOAD-FLAKY — under high machine load (the parallel fix agents spiked load avg to 10–14) it fails non-deterministically across M1/M2/M3 (a truly-inert never-executed statement trips it too; the unchanged baseline both passed and failed at different loads). The deterministic gates (fidelity/completability/invariants) are clean. Re-verify the perturbation gate under LOW load before trusting it; the oracle was left immutable.
